name: Dr Ryan Porter
focus: Earthquake seismology • Crustal and mantle tectonics • Shallow geophysics
bio: Dr. Porter's research interests are focused on using earthquake seismology to study tectonics and on applying shallow geophysics to understand water and volcanic systems. He is specifically interested in crustal and upper mantle structure, subduction zone processes, and seismic anisotropy. Much of his work focuses on studying the impact of subduction on the tectonic evolution of the North and South American Cordillera, on ground and surface water systems in Arizona, and on applications of geophysics for planetary exploration. Dr. Porter primarily teaches upper-division and graduate-level geophysics classes, as well as, introductory geology classes and seminars.
